verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a type 1 transmembrane protein with an N-terminal signal peptide and a single TM domain, anchoring it to the ER membrane. The N-terminal ectodomain contains EF-hand motifs for Ca2+ sensing. Upon ER Ca2+ depletion, STIM1 oligomerizes and translocates to ER-plasma membrane (PM) junctions, where its cytoplasmic C-terminal tail directly gates ORAI1 and other store-operated Ca2+ channels at the PM. This TM-anchored state at the PM is documented by multiple structural and functional studies. The protein reaches the outer leaflet via its own TM domain and is functionally engaged at the plasma membrane during store-operated Ca2+ entry signaling.
